Your submission will be rejected if any are missing. `resolved` = the agent fixed it in this window. `wontfix` = the agent declined with a reasonable justification; stop flagging. `still_open` = you can see IN THIS WINDOW that it's not addressed or the fix is incomplete. `out_of_scope` = the prior relates to work outside this window — you cannot tell from here. Use `out_of_scope` (not `still_open`) whenever the honest answer is 'not in my window'. Do NOT re-emit the finding in `findings` — `findings` is for NEW issues in this window only.

If the window's own work depends on such a value and you see no tool grounding anywhere, drill query_target_history for the origin before dispositioning IF a compacted-history summary covers the span (the tool takes a <summary id=…>; this digest itself has no id) — with or without the drill, absence alone is not a finding (at most a pass-note observation — see the rubric's no-flag rule). > ## What I did
> 
> **1–2. Verified live state and found the staleness.** Current `main` is at `f8845eb8` with the `pipeline` pin at `334cc74` — confirmed, with all of #66–#69 merged and no PRs left open from the closeout. The `6c87890 → 334cc74` bump (#68) is not a bare pin swap: it carries **three** FLITS commits — #147 (regenerate joint summary), #146 (mahi docstring), and **#148 (derive `DEFAULT_OUT_DIR` from the repo, not a hardcoded laptop path)**. That last one is what made the reproducibility docs genuinely stale.
> 
> **3. Opened [PR #70](https://github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pull/70)** — docs-only (`REPRODUCE.md` +23/−12, `repro_manifest.csv` +3/−3), `parity` running, mergeable and blocked on the human review gate as expected. Corrections:
> - Three stale *current-state* claims updated `6c87890 → 334cc74` (the "currently pinned submodule" line, hazard 1's parity-verification note, hazard 5's header).
> - **Hazard 5** rew
> …[778 chars elided]…
> ified 2026-07-09" lines verbatim. Those are historical provenance of work performed at that pin; rewriting them to `334cc74` would assert a clone/parity verification that nobody ran at the new pin. My edits add the forward-looking correction alongside, rather than overwriting the record — and the notes say so plainly.
> 
> **4. The `.gitmodules`/fork-network-SHA fragility** is left as a separate tracked follow-up, untouched here (the docs-only cleanup didn't need to invoke it).
> 
> **5. The three parked `@decision` items** (nickname↔TNS ownership, `FRB 20240122A` TNS registration, near-miss manuscript sentence) are unchanged — left for you.
> 
> Saved locally too: [REPRODUCE.md]({{artifact:6cd937c4-05ca-4b9b-8041-d00fd860ba7b}}) and [repro_manifest.csv]({{artifact:ddd409e2-9203-4d92-85d7-d3cb44e2358e}}).
> 
> One thing to note on merge order: PR #70 edits `REPRODUCE.md`, and if any other PR touches the same file before #70 lands, #70 will need a rebase. Right now it's the only open PR, so it's clean.
⟦digest:4a3aed2e:end⟧

Faber2026 #68.)' and the body states the DEFAULT_OUT_DIR fix 'is now in the pinned submodule (`334cc74`, verified...)' (lines ~321-347, read via read_file). But the unedited 'Suggested next steps' section later in the same file (lines ~403-406) still says: '**Hazards (5) and (6) are open.** (5) is a small submodule fix: make DEFAULT_OUT_DIR repo-relative in the two galaxies/v2_0/ modules, and add the missing build_unified_records to sightline_budget.py's fallback import.' This directly contradicts the just-updated hazard-5 status (DEFAULT_OUT_DIR fix is done, per the agent's own edit) and was not caught by the agent's own staleness grep (which only searched for '6c87890'/'current pin'/'bumped past' patterns, none of which match this passage). A reader relying on the next-steps checklist would be told to still fix something the hazard-5 section says is already fixed.

Acknowledge in one line and make the fix (or rebut in one line if a finding is wrong). Don't restate or narrate your evaluation.
</verification_findings>

For every number/identifier/citation the agent asserted, find the tool_result that produced it. Materially contradicts it (wrong sign/magnitude/entity/conclusion) → fail. A claimed ACTION with no corresponding tool activity in the traceable history (drill first — actions get the same pre-window escape as values) → fail. Cannot trace it in-window → NOT a finding (values routinely originate before the window; drill query_target_history when the origin is load-bearing) — EXCEPT an EXTERNAL citation/identifier (literature, database, accession — NOT the agent's own earlier session artifacts, which follow the value rules) presented as retrieved or established that traces nowhere in session or history: that stays a finding (warn in prose; fail in a saved artifact; if the history tool is unavailable, the source scan is truncated, or the claimed origin's span is elided in the excerpt, warn — unverifiable). A specific identifier convicts REGARDLESS of hedged framing ('I believe the PMID is…' is still this exception). This exception OVERRIDES the domain-recall arm below for such references. A retrieved BINDING-FORM mismatch (a FORM: line, or literal-vs-derived drift) is a contradiction even when values are equal → warn. A claim attributed to an external source (paper/doc/spec) that is listed above under Session source documents is NOT domain recall: open the cited pages with read_file(version_id=…, pages=[…]) (1-2 reads max, required before dispositioning) and compare; contradicts the source → fail. If the referenced source is NOT in the session, it IS domain recall — do not flag it (EXCEPT a reference carrying a specific external-work identifier — REGARDLESS of framing; see the exception above; it stays a finding when it traces nowhere). Rounding, precision, or format differences in PROSE that don't change what the reader would do are NOT findings; artifact contents (saved files) get the strict bar, and an artifact headline contradicted by its own data is fail, not warn. Scope: claims PRESENTED in this window — their grounding may live earlier in the session; drill before convicting on origin. Set msg_idx to the 0-based window index of the claim's message. If a finding is about one of the artifacts-written above, set artifact_version_id to that version_id; leave unset for prose-only findings. Return via submit_output; verdict=pass is fine if clean.

You can do all your work first (code, analysis, file creation), then call submit_output as your final action to report your structured results.

import subprocess
path = None
res = None
# get artifact path

p = host.artifact_path("2e35305b-7fbd-4205-b7f8-4dba5d22d745")
with open(p) as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
print(len(lines))
for i in range(280, 360):
    print(i+1, lines[i], end='')

306    - `freya_dsa_gamma_summary.pdf` (freya = FRB 20230325A) — **resolved** to the
307      producing function `plot_subband_gamma_summary` in
308      `scintillation/scint_analysis/plotting.py`, confirmed by commit `18fbe98`
309      ("freya DSA gamma(nu) summary — manuscript export", FLITS #130, run at
310      pinned submodule `25b8cc6` against `scintillation/configs/bursts/freya_dsa.yaml`
311      Lorentzian fits). The function takes a caller-supplied `save_path` and no
312      committed CLI script passes the `freya_dsa_gamma_summary` stem, so it is
313      `candidate`, not `yes` — the author should confirm/commit the driver.
314    - `whitney_multiplicity.pdf` (whitney = FRB 20220310F) — **unresolved.** It
315      was committed as a binary copy-in (commit `438825f`), not by any script in
316      the tree. Commit `b96aa29` frames it as whitney's 1-component multiplicity
317      demo (α rails to 1.5 under the [1.5, 6.0] prior; whitney stays 2-component
318      canonical at α≈5.1). Almost certainly a local/HPC `burstfit` run that was
319      never committed. Needs author confirmation.
320 
321 5. **Two `galaxies/v2_0/` modules defaulted their output to a hardcoded personal
322    Overleaf path — and one of them was not saved by its `run_command`. FIXED at
323    the current pin. (Code fix landed as FLITS #148, carried into this repo by the
324    `6c87890 → 334cc74` pin bump, Faber2026 #68.)**
325 
326    Hazard 3 fixed `plot_association_cards.py`. It did not fix its neighbours:
327 
328    - `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_halo_grid.py:59`
329    - `galaxies/v2_0/systems_figures.py:76`
330 
331    both set `DEFAULT_OUT_DIR = "/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/overleaf/Faber2026/figures"`.
332    `sightline_halo_grid.py` is harmless *only* because its `run_command` passes
333    `--out-dir ../figures` explicitly. `systems_figures.py`'s command did not, so
334    running it as documented exits **0** while writing `clusters_icm.*` and
335    `galaxies_cgm.*` into a directory that exists on exactly one laptop — silently
336    outside the repository, and on any other machine into a freshly `makedirs`'d
337    path nobody will look in. This was found on 2026-07-09 by executing the
338    command and then noticing the six modified files in the *Overleaf* checkout
339    (restored). **FLITS PR #148** replaced both defaults with
340    `os.path.join(os.path.dirname(_REPO), "figures")` — the same `_REPO`-derived
341    form hazard 3 used — and that fix is now in the pinned submodule (`334cc74`,
342    verified: `DEFAULT_OUT_DIR` is repo-derived at
343    `sightline_halo_grid.py:63` and `systems_figures.py:80`). A bare run therefore
344    lands `clusters_icm.*` / `galaxies_cgm.*` inside the repository. The manifest's
345    `run_command` still passes `--out-dir ../figures` explicitly, so the documented
346    invocation was already safe and remains so. The undeclared ordering dependency
347    below is unaffected by #148 and still stands.
348 
349    The same run exposed an **undeclared ordering dependency**: `systems_figures.py`
350    reads `pipeline/results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.csv`, which nothing in
351    the manifest produced before it. That CSV comes from `sightline_budget`, which
352    in turn only runs in **module** form — as a script its direct-execution import
353    fallback (`galaxies/foreground/sightline_budget.py:61-65`) imports
354    `MASS_PRIORITY` but drops `build_unified_records`, so line 494 raises
355    `NameError`. Both are fixed in the manifest's commands and neither was
356    detectable by reading a `savefig` line.
357 
358 6. **Seven staged figures are not reproducible from a clone by anyone.**
359    `chime_subband_compare.py`, `joint_ladder/_subband_tau_validation.py` and
360    `plot_jointmodel_montage.py` read from `/central/scratch/jfaber/flits-runs/…`,
